label: Caution
description: Integral part of prudence avoiding evils mixed with apparent goods.

label: Circumspection
description: Integral part of prudence attending to relevant circumstances.

label: Command
description: The principal act of prudence in commanding action.

label: Common Good
description: The communal end to which public prudence is ordered.

label: Gift of Counsel
description: The corresponding gift of the Holy Ghost perfecting prudence.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from counsel as an act of deliberation.

label: Covetousness
description: Vice named as the chief origin of counterfeit prudence-like forms in Q55 a8.

label: Craftiness
description: Counterfeit prudence using fictitious or counterfeit means.

label: Docility
description: Integral part of prudence disposing a person to learn from others.

label: Domestic Prudence
description: Subjective part of prudence governing the household.
disambiguation_note: The source title uses 'domestic economy'; the normalized concept remains Domestic Prudence.

label: Euboulia
description: Potential part of prudence concerned with good counsel.
disambiguation_note: Keep the transliterated term because the tract names it directly.

label: False Prudence
description: A prudence-like likeness directed to an apparent good rather than true good.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from craftiness and prudence of the flesh.

label: Foresight
description: Integral part of prudence ordering means fittingly to the end.

label: Fraud
description: Execution of craftiness more properly by deeds.

label: Solicitude about the Future
description: Needlessly forestalling solicitude proper to a future time.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from lawful prudential foresight.

label: Gnome
description: Potential part of prudence judging according to higher principles in exceptional cases.
disambiguation_note: Keep separate from synesis and from prudence generally.

label: Guile
description: Execution of craftiness in words or deeds.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from fraud, which is more properly by deeds.

label: Imprudence
description: Vice opposed to prudence through lack or contrary movement of reason.

label: Inconstancy
description: Defect of command withdrawing from a good purpose.

label: Understanding or Intelligence
description: Integral part of prudence concerning right estimate of first practical principles.
disambiguation_note: Do not collapse into the distinct gift of understanding from elsewhere in the corpus.

label: Lust
description: Vice named as chief origin of defects of prudence in Q53 a6.

label: Memory
description: Integral part of prudence concerning past experience.

label: Beatitude of Mercy
description: The fifth beatitude associated here with the gift of counsel.

label: Military Prudence
description: Subjective part of prudence repelling hostile attacks for the common good.

label: Negligence
description: Lack of due solicitude treated as pertaining to imprudence.

label: Political Prudence
description: Subjective part of prudence in subjects directing themselves in obedience.
disambiguation_note: Article 50.2 uses the title 'political economy' in this edition; normalize the concept label as Political Prudence.

label: Practical Reason
description: Reason as ordered to action and counsel.
disambiguation_note: Do not collapse into reason_faculty when the tract is specifically distinguishing practical from speculative reason.

label: Precipitation or Temerity
description: Defect of counsel contained under imprudence.

label: Prudence
description: The cardinal virtue of right practical reason in things to be done.

label: Prudence of the Flesh
description: Counterfeit prudence that treats bodily goods as the last end of life.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from false prudence and from craftiness.

label: Reason
description: The rational cognitive faculty in which prudence is located.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from Reasoning, the integral part of prudence in Q49 a5.

label: Reasoning
description: Integral part of prudence in comparing one thing with another during counsel.
disambiguation_note: Distinguish from reason_faculty, the cognitive power in which prudence resides.

label: Regnative Prudence
description: Subjective part of prudence belonging to rulers of a city or kingdom.

label: Shrewdness
description: Integral part of prudence signifying apt discovery of the fitting course.
disambiguation_note: The source sometimes glosses this through eustochia; keep the prudence-part label stable.

label: Solicitude
description: Prompt practical watchfulness treated as belonging to prudence.
disambiguation_note: Keep separate from unlawful solicitude about temporal or future things in Q55.

label: Synesis
description: Potential part of prudence concerned with good judgment under common law.

label: Solicitude about Temporal Things
description: Unlawful solicitude seeking temporal things as end or with undue anxiety.
disambiguation_note: Keep distinct from solicitude as a prudential act in Q47 a9.

label: Thoughtlessness
description: Defect of right judgment treated under imprudence.

label: Counterfeit Prudence Forms
description: Editorial grouping node for prudence-like but misdirected forms.

label: Gift of Counsel Tract
description: Editorial grouping node for the gift of counsel material.

label: Integral Parts of Prudence
description: Editorial grouping node for prudence's integral parts.

label: Potential Parts of Prudence
description: Editorial grouping node for prudence's potential parts.

label: Prudence Core Tract
description: Editorial tract marker for prudence itself in QQ. 47–48.

label: Defects Opposed to Prudence
description: Editorial grouping node for imprudence and allied defects.

label: Precepts of Prudence
description: Editorial grouping node for the precept material in Q56.

label: Subjective Parts of Prudence
description: Editorial grouping node for prudence's subjective parts.
